content:
# Exodus Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~33v–34r (PDF pp. 65–66); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 45–46 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_005_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_005_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_005_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_005_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** And afterward Mosheh and Aharon came and they said to Par'oh: "Thus says YHWH, the God of Yisra'el: 'Shalach et-ammi [Send out my people] and let them make-a-feast to me in the wilderness.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַחַר בָּאוּ מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן (ve'akhar ba'u Mosheh ve'Aharon). LXX (Vaticanus): καὶ μετὰ ταῦτα εἰσῆλθεν Μωυσῆς καὶ Ααρων πρὸς Φαραω ("and after these things Moses and Aaron went in to Pharao") -- adds πρὸς Φαραω where MT has only אֶל־פַּרְעֹה.

**2** And Par'oh said: "Mi YHWH [Who is YHWH] that I should listen to his voice to send out Yisra'el? I do not know YHWH, and also Yisra'el I will not send out."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads מִי יְהוָה אֲשֶׁר אֶשְׁמַע בְּקֹלוֹ. LXX: τίς ἐστιν οὗ εἰσακούσομαι τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ("who is he whose voice I shall hear") -- renders YHWH with a relative pronoun rather than the divine name.

**3** And they said: "The God of the Ivrim [Hebrews] has met with us. Let us go, please, a journey of three days into the wilderness, and let us sacrifice to YHWH our God, lest he strike us with pestilence or with the sword."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֱלֹהֵי הָעִבְרִים נִקְרָא עָלֵינוּ (niqra 'aleinu, "has been called upon us / has met with us"). LXX: ὁ θεὸς τῶν Εβραίων προσκέκληται ἡμᾶς ("the God of the Hebrews has summoned us") -- uses a passive/middle form paralleling the Hebrew niphal.

**4** And the king of Mitsrayim said to them: "Why, Mosheh and Aharon, do you cause the people to neglect from their labors? Go to your sivlot [burdens]!"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לָמָּה מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן תַּפְרִיעוּ (tafri'u, hiphil, "cause to neglect/loosen"). LXX: ἵνα τί Μωυσῆ καὶ Ααρων διαστρέφετε τὸν λαόν ("why do you turn the people aside") -- renders with διαστρέφετε ("pervert/turn aside") rather than a causative of neglect. Sivlot (סִבְלֹת): forced-labor burdens.

**5** And Par'oh said: "Behold, many now [are] the people of the land, and you would cause them to rest from their sivlot [burdens]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הֶן־רַבִּים עַתָּה עַם הָאָרֶץ וְהִשְׁבַּתֶּם אֹתָם מִסִּבְלֹתָם (vehishbatem, hiphil of shavat, "and you would cause-them-to-cease"). LXX adds: πορευέσθω εἰς τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ ("let [each one] go to his works").

**6** And Par'oh commanded on that day the nogsim [taskmasters/drivers] among the people and their shotrim [foremen/officers], saying:

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֶת־הַנֹּגְשִׂים (ha-nogsim) and וְאֶת־שֹׁטְרָיו (ve'et-shotrav). Nogsim (נֹגְשִׂים): those who press/drive; shotrim (שֹׁטְרִים): officials who kept records and enforced quotas. LXX: τοῖς ἐργοδιώκταις ("work-pursuers") and γραμματεῦσιν ("scribes").

**7** "You shall not continue to give teven [straw/stubble] to the people to make the leveним [bricks] as yesterday [and] the-day-before. They -- let them go and let them gather teven [straw] for themselves.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא תֹאסִפוּן לָתֵת תֶּבֶן (teven) לָעָם לִלְבֹּן הַלְּבֵנִים (lilbon ha-levenim, "to brick the bricks"). LXX: ἄχυρον ("chaff") for teven; πλινθεύειν τὰς πλίνθους ("to brick the bricks") -- mirrors the Hebrew cognate construction.

**8** And the matkonet [quota/tally] of the levenim [bricks] which they [were] making yesterday [and] the-day-before, you shall place upon them; you shall not diminish from it, for they [are] idle -- 'al-ken [therefore] they [are] crying out, saying: 'Let us go, let us sacrifice to our God.'

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־מַתְכֹּנֶת הַלְּבֵנִים (matkonet ha-levenim). Matkonet (מַתְכֹּנֶת): fixed measure/quota. כִּי־נִרְפִּים הֵם (ki-nirpim hem, "for idle/slack [are] they"). LXX: τὴν σύνταξιν τῆς πλινθείας ("the arrangement of brick-making"); σχολάζουσιν ("they have leisure").

**9** Let the labor be heavy upon the men, and let them do it; and let them not look upon divrei-shaqer [words of falsehood]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads תִּכְבַּד הָעֲבֹדָה עַל־הָאֲנָשִׁים (tikhbad ha-avodah, "let the labor be heavy"). אַל־יִשְׁעוּ בְּדִבְרֵי־שָׁקֶר (al-yish'u be-divrei-shaqer, "let them not gaze at words of lie"). LXX: μὴ μεριμνάτωσαν ἐν λόγοις κενοῖς ("let them not be anxious about empty words") -- renders shaqer as κενοῖς ("empty") rather than "false."

**10** And the nogsim [taskmasters] of the people and their shotrim [foremen] went out, and they said to the people, saying: "Thus says Par'oh: 'I am not giving to you teven [straw].

**11** You -- go, take for yourselves teven [straw] from wherever you find [it], for there is not diminished from your labor a thing.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אַתֶּם לְכוּ קְחוּ לָכֶם תֶּבֶן מֵאֲשֶׁר תִּמְצָאוּ (me'asher timtsa'u, "from where you will find"). כִּי אֵין נִגְרָע מֵעֲבֹדַתְכֶם דָּבָר (ein nigra, niphal, "there is not diminished").

**12** And the people scattered in all the land of Mitsrayim to gather qash [stubble] for the teven [straw].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לְקֹשֵׁשׁ קַשׁ לַתֶּבֶן (leqoshesh qash la-teven, "to gather stubble for the straw"). Qash (קַשׁ): dry stubble, distinct from teven (תֶּבֶן, chopped straw). LXX: καλάμην εἰς ἄχυρα ("stubble for straw").

**13** And the nogsim [taskmasters] [were] pressing, saying: "Complete your works, the matter of a day in its day, as when there was teven [straw]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַנֹּגְשִׂים אָצִים (atsim, "pressing/urging"). דְּבַר־יוֹם בְּיוֹמוֹ (devar-yom be-yomo, "the matter of a day in its day"). כַּאֲשֶׁר בִּהְיוֹת הַתֶּבֶן (ka'asher bihyot ha-teven, "as when there was the straw").

**14** And the shotrim [foremen] of the sons of Yisra'el, whom the nogsim [taskmasters] of Par'oh had placed over them, were beaten, saying: "Why have you not completed your choq [prescribed-portion] to make-bricks as yesterday [and] the-day-before, both yesterday and today?"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֻּכּוּ שֹׁטְרֵי בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (vayukku shotrei, hophal, "were beaten"). Choq (חֹק): prescribed portion/statute. לִלְבֹּן (lilbon): to make bricks. The phrase גַּם־תְּמוֹל גַּם־הַיּוֹם (gam-temol gam-ha-yom, "both yesterday and today") reinforces the accusation. LXX: ἐμαστιγώθησαν ("were whipped") -- more specific than the Hebrew "beaten."

**15** And the shotrim [foremen] of the sons of Yisra'el came and they cried out to Par'oh, saying: "Why do you do thus to your servants?

**16** Teven [Straw] is not given to your servants, and levenim [bricks] they say to us: 'Make!' And behold, your servants [are] beaten, and the sin [is on] your people."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads תֶּבֶן אֵין נִתָּן לַעֲבָדֶיךָ וּלְבֵנִים אֹמְרִים לָנוּ עֲשׂוּ (u-levenim omrim lanu asu, "and bricks they say to us: Make!"). וְחָטָאת עַמֶּךָ (ve-khatat amekha, "and the sin [is on] your people"). LXX: ἀδικήσεις οὖν τὸν λαόν σου ("you will then wrong your people") -- interprets the cryptic Hebrew phrase as a direct accusation.

**17** And he said: "Idle [are] you! Idle! 'Al-ken [Therefore] you [are] saying: 'Let us go, let us sacrifice to YHWH.'

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads נִרְפִּים אַתֶּם נִרְפִּים (nirpim atem nirpim, "idle [are] you, idle") -- emphatic repetition. LXX: σχολάζετε σχολασταί ἐστε ("you idle, you are idlers").

**18** And now, go, labor! And teven [straw] will not be given to you, and the matkonet [quota] of levenim [bricks] you shall give."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעַתָּה לְכוּ עִבְדוּ וְתֶבֶן לֹא־יִנָּתֵן לָכֶם וְתֹכֶן לְבֵנִים תִּתֵּנוּ (ve-tokhen levenim titenu). Here tokhen (תֹּכֶן), a variant form of matkonet, means "measure/quota."

**19** And the shotrim [foremen] of the sons of Yisra'el saw them in evil, saying: "You shall not diminish from your levenim [bricks], the matter of a day in its day."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּרְאוּ שֹׁטְרֵי בְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל אֹתָם בְּרָע (be-ra', "in evil/distress"). לֵאמֹר לֹא־תִגְרְעוּ מִלְּבֵנֵיכֶם (lo tigre'u mil-leveneikhem, "you shall not diminish from your bricks"). LXX: ἑώρων... ἐν κακοῖς ("they saw... in evils").

**20** And they met Mosheh and Aharon standing to meet them as they came out from Par'oh.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּפְגְּעוּ אֶת־מֹשֶׁה וְאֶת־אַהֲרֹן נִצָּבִים לִקְרָאתָם (nitsavim liqratam, "standing to meet them"). The participle nitsavim suggests Mosheh and Aharon were waiting deliberately.

**21** And they said to them: "Let YHWH look upon you and let [him] judge, for you have caused our scent to stink in the eyes of Par'oh and in the eyes of his servants, to give a sword in their hand to kill us."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הִבְאַשְׁתֶּם אֶת־רֵיחֵנוּ (hiv'ashtem et-reichenu, hiphil, "you have caused our scent to stink") -- vivid idiom for ruining reputation. בְּעֵינֵי פַרְעֹה וּבְעֵינֵי עֲבָדָיו (be'einei, "in the eyes of"). LXX: ἐβδελύξατε τὴν ὀσμὴν ἡμῶν ("you have made our smell abominable").

**22** And Mosheh returned to YHWH and said: "My Lord, why have you done-evil to this people? Why this [that] you have sent me?

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיָּשָׁב מֹשֶׁה אֶל־יְהוָה (vayashav, "and he returned"). לָמָה הֲרֵעֹתָה לָעָם הַזֶּה (ha-re'otah, hiphil, "have you done-evil/caused-harm"). LXX: κύριε, διὰ τί ἐκάκωσας τὸν λαὸν τοῦτον ("Lord, why have you mistreated this people").

**23** And from-then [I] came to Par'oh to speak in your name, he has done-evil to this people; and delivering -- you have not delivered your people."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּמֵאָז בָּאתִי אֶל־פַּרְעֹה לְדַבֵּר בִּשְׁמֶךָ הֵרַע לָעָם הַזֶּה (hera', hiphil, "he has done-evil"). וְהַצֵּל לֹא־הִצַּלְתָּ אֶת־עַמֶּךָ (ve-hatsel lo-hitsalta, infinitive absolute + finite verb for emphasis, "and delivering you have not delivered"). LXX: καὶ ἐξ οὗ πεπόρευμαι... οὐκ ἐρρύσω τὸν λαόν σου ("and from when I went... you have not rescued your people").

---

## Key Terminology — Exodus 5

| Hebrew | Transliteration | Literal Meaning | Verses |
|---|---|---|---|
| שַׁלַּח אֶת־עַמִּי | shalach et-ammi | send out my people | 1 |
| מִי יְהוָה | mi YHWH | who [is] YHWH? | 2 |
| נֹגְשִׂים | nogsim | taskmasters/drivers | 6, 10, 13, 14 |
| שֹׁטְרִים | shotrim | foremen/officers | 6, 10, 14, 15, 19 |
| תֶּבֶן | teven | straw/chopped straw | 7, 10, 11, 12, 13, 16, 18 |
| לְבֵנִים | levenim | bricks | 7, 8, 14, 16, 18, 19 |
| מַתְכֹּנֶת / תֹּכֶן | matkonet / tokhen | quota/prescribed measure | 8, 18 |
| קַשׁ | qash | stubble (dry) | 12 |
| נִרְפִּים | nirpim | idle/slack | 8, 17 |
| סִבְלֹת | sivlot | forced-labor burdens | 4, 5 |
| חֹק | choq | prescribed portion | 14 |
| הִבְאַשְׁתֶּם אֶת־רֵיחֵנוּ | hiv'ashtem et-reichenu | you caused our scent to stink | 21 |
| הֵרַע | hera' | he did evil / caused harm | 22, 23 |
| הַצֵּל לֹא־הִצַּלְתָּ | hatsel lo-hitsalta | delivering you have not delivered | 23 |

---

## Variant Notes Summary

| Verse | MT (Leningrad Codex) | LXX (Vaticanus) | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | אֶל־פַּרְעֹה ("to Par'oh") | πρὸς Φαραω ("to Pharao") | Equivalent |
| 2 | מִי יְהוָה ("who [is] YHWH") | τίς ἐστιν οὗ ("who is [he] whose...") | LXX avoids divine name |
| 4 | תַּפְרִיעוּ ("cause to neglect") | διαστρέφετε ("turn aside/pervert") | Semantic shift in LXX |
| 5 | וְהִשְׁבַּתֶּם ("and you would cause to cease") | LXX adds "let each go to his works" | Expansion in LXX |
| 9 | דִבְרֵי־שָׁקֶר ("words of falsehood") | λόγοις κενοῖς ("empty words") | LXX softens "falsehood" to "empty" |
| 14 | וַיֻּכּוּ ("were beaten") | ἐμαστιγώθησαν ("were whipped") | LXX more specific |
| 16 | וְחָטָאת עַמֶּךָ ("and the sin [of] your people") | ἀδικήσεις τὸν λαόν σου ("you will wrong your people") | LXX reinterprets as direct accusation |
| 21 | הִבְאַשְׁתֶּם אֶת־רֵיחֵנוּ ("caused our scent to stink") | ἐβδελύξατε τὴν ὀσμὴν ἡμῶν ("made our smell abominable") | Equivalent idiom |
